**Note:** `append_path_shadow_projection_test.dart` (325 lines) has no one-to-one impl mirror — it tests the append-path behaviour of `shadow_projection.dart`. The "one test file per source file" guideline allows for this only when the extra file tests a clearly distinct subsystem. Since `shadow_projection.dart` already has `shadow_projection_test.dart`, the append-path tests could be merged into that file (combined 594 lines, still within bounds) unless the separation is deliberate for readability.

## File size / split opportunities

- No implementation file in `lib/features/agents/projection/` exceeds 500 lines; all are appropriately sized.
- No test file exceeds 715 lines. No file exceeds the 1000-line hard limit.
- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/projection/derived_agent_state_test.dart` (715 lines) — the file is large because it covers four distinct concerns: (1) `deriveAgentState` watermark fold, (2) `deriveAgentState` slot fold, (3) `compareDerivedAgentState` mismatch/error, and (4) `reconcileAgentState` merge semantics. These could be split into `derived_agent_state_fold_test.dart` and `derived_agent_state_reconcile_test.dart` at roughly equal sizes, but this is a quality-of-life split rather than a hard requirement.
- [ ] **[LOW]** Consider merging `append_path_shadow_projection_test.dart` (325 lines) into `shadow_projection_test.dart` (269 lines, total ~594) under a separate `group('append-path', ...)` block, satisfying the one-test-file-per-source-file rule. If the separation is intentional for CI shard locality keep as-is.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[LOW]** `shadow_projection_test.dart:156` — `expect(report.error, isNotNull)` when a cycle is injected. This is an appropriate signal-presence check for the error field, but the error string is predictable (it starts with `'ProjectionCycleException'`). Adding `expect(report.error, contains('ProjectionCycleException'))` would make the assertion stronger without being brittle.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `derived_agent_state_test.dart:397` — `expect(report.error, isNotNull)` when a duplicate-id exception is injected. Same pattern as above; consider `expect(report.error, contains('DuplicateEventIdException'))`.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `projection_convergence_test.dart` (88 lines) — the single Glados test drives `compareDerivedAgentState` and checks `equivalent == true` for a well-formed log. It verifies `report.error == null` and `report.fieldMismatches.isEmpty` indirectly through the `equivalent` getter. No weak assertions; no issue beyond the small scope.

- [ ] **[MED]** `append_path_shadow_projection_test.dart` — two Glados tests at lines 256 and 281 each re-declare similar generator extensions. The generators are small (3–4 lines each), but they partially overlap with `_AnyProjectionDag` in `projection_test_fixtures.dart`. Neither is in three or more files (only this file), so the convention allows per-file generators here. No action required, but note for future housekeeping if a third file adopts the same shape.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `agent_event_adapter_test.dart:30` — `glados.Glados(glados.any.messageKind).test('maps every message kind', ...)` verifies that `agentEventsFromLog` maps every `AgentMessageKind` to an `AgentEventKind`. The property uses `glados.any.messageKind` (a `choose` generator over all enum values). After mapping, the test asserts `events.length == 1` and that the kind is non-null. A stronger assertion would verify the actual `AgentEventKind` value produced for each `AgentMessageKind`, particularly for the `system` kind which maps to `AgentEventKind.message` (i.e., confirm the mapping is not accidentally silent). This is a minor quality improvement.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `input_frontier_test.dart` — tests at lines 119 and 131 (Glados) cover "frontier is a pure function of the log set" and "retraction removes the entry". The two remaining example-based tests in the `test('hard delete …')` and `test('re-capture after retraction …')` groups (if present) are adequate. No weak assertions observed.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

The projection subdir already has one of the densest Glados test suites in the repository (42 Glados invocations across 14 test files). The following assessment is therefore focused on genuine gaps only.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `derived_agent_state.dart:362–393` — `reconcileAgentState` merge semantics (watermark `max`, slot `derived ?? cache`). This function is tested by example-based tests in `derived_agent_state_test.dart` (approximately lines 560–715). A Glados property over arbitrary `(derived, cache)` watermark pairs asserting that the output always satisfies `max(derived, cache)` for each watermark field would be a genuine property-test addition. The function is pure and the invariant is algebraic (`max` idempotence, commutativity, associativity). Estimated generator complexity: moderate (two nullable `DateTime?` fields per watermark, ~5 watermarks). However, the existing example-based tests cover all branch combinations (`derived > cache`, `cache > derived`, both null, one null), so the expected Glados shrink benefit is low. Flagged as **[LOW]** rather than a hard gap.

- No other genuine Glados gaps identified. All pure functions with structural inputs (canonical order, projection, compaction plan, checkpoint selection, input frontier, content digest, capture) have property tests with ≥120 runs and strong algebraic invariants.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/agents/projection/derived_agent_state.dart:157–169` — `_primaryActiveLinkTarget`: the function filters active links (`deletedAt == null`) by type and `fromId == agentId`, then calls `.orderedPrimaryFirst().first`. The "most-recent-wins" tiebreak via `orderedPrimaryFirst` is tested in `derived_agent_state_test.dart` via the Glados fold scenario, but the edge case where all matching links are soft-deleted (returning `null`) is only exercised implicitly through the "no links" scenario. An explicit test asserting `activeTaskId == null` when all `AgentTaskLink`s have `deletedAt != null` would close this gap.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/features/agents/projection/derived_agent_state.dart:398–401` — `_laterOf(null, null)` returns `null` (both branches return early). This trivial path is exercised indirectly by the Glados fold tests whenever no markers exist. An explicit `test('_laterOf returns null when both are null', ...)` would be clean documentation, but since `_laterOf` is private and thoroughly exercised via `reconcileAgentState` tests it is low risk.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/agents/projection/shadow_projection.dart:119` — the `liveHeadId == null` branch returning `mismatch` when `heads` is non-empty is tested at `shadow_projection_test.dart` line ~130 (`'non-empty projection but null live head → mismatch'`). Confirmed covered.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/agents/projection/checkpoint_selection.dart:100–107` — the tie-breaking logic for concurrent summaries with equal coverage (`coverage == bestCoverage && event.id < active.id`) is tested by an example-based test in `checkpoint_selection_test.dart` ("concurrent summaries: lowest id wins"). The Glados property at line 79 drives `selectActiveCheckpoint` with arbitrary DAGs but does not include summary events (the generator produces only `AgentEventKind.message` events). A Glados extension that randomly sprinkles `summary` events into the DAG would exercise the tiebreak path combinatorially. Given the existing example-based coverage this is a **[LOW]** gap.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/agents/projection/compaction_summary.dart:59` — `selectActiveSummary` is tested in `compaction_summary_test.dart` including stale-digest and multi-checkpoint scenarios. All branch paths appear covered. No gap.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/features/agents/projection/input_frontier.dart:65–79` — the `ops.sort` + fold loop handles simultaneous `createdAt` ties via `tiebreak` (link/message id). The Glados property in `input_frontier_test.dart` includes "same timestamp, different ids" but the exact tiebreak order (ascending by id) is only spot-checked. A targeted test with two ops sharing an exact timestamp but different ids and asserting the later-id op wins is worth adding if the tiebreak direction ever changes.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/projection/compaction_plan_test.dart:31,47,62,74,95` — Five Glados properties each at `numRuns: 300`, totalling 1 500 iterations. These test pure functions (`computeCompactionPlan`) with no I/O. The high run count is appropriate for a pure function, but `300` is well above the 200-run guideline. If CI wall-time becomes a concern, reducing to `numRuns: 200` (still enough to exercise most combinatorial paths for a compact pure function) would cut this file's Glados cost by 33 % with minimal coverage impact.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/projection/input_capture_test.dart:24,43,56,67,77,242,260,278,298,313` — Ten Glados properties at `numRuns: 250` each (plus one at `numRuns: 200` at line 87), totalling ~2 700 iterations. These test pure input-capture functions with no I/O. The 250-run level is above the 200-run guideline. Reducing to `200` across all ten would cut ~500 iterations (roughly 17 %) from this file alone with negligible coverage reduction for these well-bounded pure functions.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/agents/projection/input_frontier_test.dart:121,134` — Two Glados properties at `numRuns: 300` each (600 total iterations) for pure frontier-computation functions. Same reasoning as `compaction_plan_test.dart`: reducing to `200` would save 200 iterations while the pure function logic is fully covered by the generator's combinatorial depth at that level.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/features/agents/projection/compaction_summary_test.dart:42,83` and `derived_agent_state_test.dart:307` — Three Glados properties at `numRuns: 250` each. All test pure functions; the same guideline-reduction from 250→200 would save ~150 iterations across the three files.

- [ ] **[LOW]** No `pumpAndSettle`, `Future.delayed`, real `Timer`, `Stream.periodic`, or `sleep` calls found in any projection test file. No fake-time violations. No per-test DB open/close or heavy mock-rebuild patterns (all projection tests are pure-function Glados or example-based with no I/O).

---

## Summary

- **Zero** implementation files in `lib/features/agents/projection/` exceed 500 lines; the subdir is exemplarily sized.
- **Zero** oversized test files (none exceed 715 lines, all under the 1000-line hard limit).
- The projection subdir has **42 Glados invocations** across 14 test files — one of the densest in the codebase. No genuine property-test gap found.
- **3 weak tests** identified: two `isNotNull` checks on error fields (should assert the exception type string) and one adapter mapping assertion that could verify the specific target kind.
- **Biggest opportunity:** add an explicit test for `_primaryActiveLinkTarget` with all matching links soft-deleted, and a Glados test for `reconcileAgentState` watermark-max semantics as a pure algebraic property.
- All tests comply with the fake-time policy: no `Future.delayed`, `DateTime.now()`, or `sleep` found.
- All mocks imported from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`; no inline mock classes.
